findings.title: Artifact hash mismatches do not fail readiness at runtime
findings.description: artifactIntegrityIssue() is the gate used by staticReadiness(), but it only checks schema/block headers, route self-hashes, route references, parity self-hashes, and snapshot shape. It never computes the manifest content hashes for solver routes, gap matrix, parity vectors, pools, or the pool-state snapshot. staticReadiness() separately compares config.expected*Hash values to the manifest constants, so a deployment can report ready when the imported artifact files have drifted from those manifest hashes as long as the semantic checks still pass.
findings.impact: The live API/widget solver can build routes from unpinned pool metadata or recorded quote artifacts while still passing readiness. For example, changing a pool address/fee in base-v1-pools.json or a positive quote amount in the snapshot does not trip artifactIntegrityIssue(), but those values are then used by route generation and quote replay.
findings.suggested_fix: Add runtime-safe content-hash verification for every manifest hash before returning null from artifactIntegrityIssue(). If raw file bytes are unavailable in client bundles, generate and import canonical artifact hashes during build, or hash a deterministic canonical serialization that the manifest also uses. Add regression tests that mutate each artifact class and assert staticReadiness() returns artifact_hash_mismatch.

findings.title: 78-digit amount validation still accepts values outside uint256
findings.description: The quote API accepts any integer string up to 78 digits, but many 78-digit values are greater than uint256 max. Those values pass the handler validation and are converted to BigInt. A live reserve-math route can then be selected, after which solveFameSwapAmountAsync() calls hashFameRoute()/encodeFameRoute() for uint256 fields and viem throws IntegerOutOfRangeError.
findings.impact: A public quote request such as amountIn = '9'.repeat(78) can turn an invalid user amount into an unhandled 500 instead of a controlled 400/no_safe_route response. The same boundary can surface from the widget if a user enters an extremely large decimal amount.
findings.suggested_fix: Parse amountIn once in the API, reject values greater than 2^256 - 1, and pass the parsed bigint forward. Consider sharing the same uint256 guard with the solver or route encoder so non-API callers also fail closed before ABI encoding.

findings.title: Failed wallet submission is treated as terminal revert and blocks retry
findings.description: submitApproval() and submitSwap() set localError for wallet rejection or writeContractAsync failures before any transaction hash exists. The hook then derives reverted as receipt.isError || localError !== null. FameSwapWidget maps reverted ahead of approval/ready states and disables the primary CTA, so a user who rejects the wallet prompt or hits a transient submit failure cannot retry the same quote.
findings.impact: A retryable client-side failure becomes a terminal 'Swap reverted' state. The user must change the quote inputs or otherwise force the execution key to reset before trying the same approval/swap again.
findings.suggested_fix: Separate retryable submission errors from on-chain reverts. Only set reverted for a receipt status of reverted (or an actual transaction receipt failure that should be terminal), keep write/user-rejection errors in error, clear them at the start of another submit, and allow canApprove/canSwap to remain true when the quote is still valid.

residual_risks: Reviewed the tracked branch diff statically; did not run the full test suite or live fork validation.
Generated wagmi output and large JSON artifact contents were sampled for correctness-relevant behavior rather than line-reviewed exhaustively.
testing_gaps: No readiness test mutates each artifact file while leaving headers/self-hashes valid to prove manifest content-hash drift fails closed.
No API boundary test covers amountIn values above uint256 max but within the current 78-digit length limit.
No hook/widget test covers wallet rejection or writeContractAsync failure and verifies the same quote remains retryable.
